hero.titleHighlight: business systems, websites, and AI apps
hero.description: Former technical lead in the technology department of a large internet company. I led multiple enterprise systems and managed a 20-person engineering team. Now I take projects directly as a solo studio without subcontracting, which fits clients who care about quality, speed, and long-term support.

background.title: Background
background.description: Former technical lead in the technology department of a large internet company, with multiple enterprise systems delivered and a 20-person engineering team led.

availability.title: Development Hours
availability.description: Development hours are 8:00 - 16:00 and 22:00 - 24:00 on weekdays, and 22:00 - 24:00 on weekends.

direct.title: Working Style
direct.description: Direct communication and direct development by me, with no subcontracting or outsourcing layers.

delivery.title: Delivery Standard
delivery.description: Projects follow a 4-4-2 payment rhythm and include one year of free maintenance after launch.

services.title: What I build and the stack I use
services.description: From public-facing sites to internal business systems, I prefer projects where product thinking, clean delivery, and long-term maintainability all matter.
services.stackTitle: Frontend and backend can both be handled directly
services.stackDescription: That means less coordination overhead, less information loss, and clearer accountability throughout the project.

brandSites.title: Corporate websites & brand sites
brandSites.description: For foreign-trade companies, service businesses, brands, and teams that need a stronger online presence.
brandSites.bullet1: Corporate sites, multilingual websites, service pages, and landing pages
brandSites.bullet2: Handled with SEO, content structure, conversion forms, and deployment in mind

webApps.title: Business systems
webApps.description: For internal systems where workflows, permissions, data, and operational logic are the core of the product.
webApps.bullet1: ERP, OA, CRM, finance, procurement, inventory, and sales systems
webApps.bullet2: Suitable for greenfield builds, upgrades, and legacy-system refactors

aiAutomation.title: AI applications
aiAutomation.description: Not just demo features, but AI connected to real workflows and actual business scenarios.
aiAutomation.bullet1: AI tools, retrieval Q&A, automation, and digital-human content workflows
aiAutomation.bullet2: Built to connect with existing systems, content pipelines, and operations

mvpDelivery.title: Commerce platforms & mini-programs
mvpDelivery.description: For transaction, booking, course, and recruiting products that need a full business loop.
mvpDelivery.bullet1: E-commerce sites, mini-programs, online courses, recruiting, and order systems
mvpDelivery.bullet2: Balanced across user experience, admin workflows, and later business expansion

process.title: Process
process.description: I prefer to lock boundaries, payment rhythm, and delivery timing early so the collaboration stays clean.

payment.title: 4-4-2 payment model
payment.description: 40% deposit to start, 40% in the middle, and the last 20% after launch.

analysis.title: Requirement analysis
analysis.description: Business goals, scope, and priorities are clarified first so the project does not drift during delivery.

timeline.title: Timeline confirmation
timeline.description: A more realistic schedule is set based on complexity and my current project slots.

sync.title: Weekly progress sync
sync.description: Progress, risks, and next steps are updated every week so nothing goes dark.

delivery.title: Deployment / code handoff
delivery.description: Launch, configuration, code delivery, and the practical handoff details are completed here.

maintenance.title: Maintenance & support
maintenance.description: Standard delivery includes one year of free maintenance for stability and later iteration.

advantages.title: Why I do not take low-price projects
advantages.description: This is not about acting expensive. Low-price work usually comes with unclear scope, heavy back-and-forth, and support pressure later, and I have already paid for those lessons.

support.title: One year of free maintenance is real work
support.description: Support after launch is part of the commitment, so very low pricing rarely covers the real lifecycle cost.
support.bullet1: Delivery does not stop the moment the site or system goes live
support.bullet2: If the price is pushed too low, quality usually pays for it later

direct.title: Quality is controlled personally, not through subcontracting
direct.description: Once work is subcontracted or passed around, quality and delivery details become much harder to control, and that can damage reputation as well.
direct.bullet1: Direct communication and direct implementation reduce information loss and blurred ownership
direct.bullet2: It is better to take fewer projects than compromise quality or reputation

focus.title: Time is reserved for good clients
focus.description: I would rather take fewer projects than let poor-fit work squeeze the schedule and damage delivery quality.
focus.bullet1: Keeps response time and delivery rhythm more stable
focus.bullet2: Makes room for long-term, higher-match cooperation

standard.title: The service bar is above typical low-price outsourcing
standard.description: The goal is maintainable delivery with better requirement thinking, code quality, and post-launch stability.
standard.bullet1: Not just getting features done, but making them usable long term
standard.bullet2: A better fit if you care about results more than the lowest quote

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: This section is now a two-row showcase strip with automatic horizontal scrolling, multi-image preview, and optional link jump for each case.
projects.helper: Hover to pause the two-row marquee. Click any preview to enlarge it. Arrow keys support previous and next images. Works with links can open directly.
projects.lightboxHint: Esc closes. Left / Up goes previous. Right / Down goes next.

asset.title: Enterprise Management System (Assets)
asset.description: A business backend focused on asset records, allocation, inventory checks, and permission control.

hr.title: Enterprise Management System (HR)
hr.description: An HR management system covering org structure, personnel files, approval flows, and internal collaboration.

finance.title: Enterprise Management System (Finance)
finance.description: A finance-oriented enterprise system handling reports, reconciliation, and operational data flow.

attendance.title: Attendance System
attendance.description: An internal system for scheduling, attendance rules, exception handling, and reporting.

oa.title: OA Approval Workflow
oa.description: An OA workflow system with configurable nodes, approval routing, notifications, and traceability.

orders.title: Order System
orders.description: A platform built around order flow, status management, after-sales handling, and business coordination.

workstation.title: Workstation System
workstation.description: A supporting system for workstation allocation, resource occupancy, and backend configuration.

digitalHuman.title: Digital Human Video Post-production
digitalHuman.description: A content-production project around digital human video editing, delivery, and workflow support.

course.title: Online Courses
course.description: An online course project combining course presentation, content management, learning flow, and operations.

recruiting.title: Recruiting
recruiting.description: A recruiting product covering job presentation, submission flow, admin management, and information routing.

idea.title: NextIdea
idea.description: A project in the direction of idea tooling and productized utility work, useful for showing product capability.

danduoduo.title: Foreign Trade Website
danduoduo.description: A corporate website for foreign-trade business presentation, content structure, and brand communication.

formart.title: Foreign Trade Website
formart.description: A foreign-trade website project balancing company image, information delivery, and business conversion.

reminders.direct: Remote and direct collaboration only. No on-site work and no second-hand or multi-layer subcontracting.
reminders.quality: I do not compete on price and I do not take low-quality crowd-sourced outsourcing work.
reminders.timeline: The clearer your budget, goal, scope, and timeline, the faster I can evaluate and price the work.

noGo.illegal: No gray or black-industry work such as web3, gambling, adult, violence, VPN, fraud, or public-web scraping.
noGo.bidding: No bidding projects, and no second-hand or multi-layer outsourcing.
noGo.crowd: No low-quality cheap outsourcing or crowd-work.

budget.title: Budget
budget.description: A clear budget saves a lot of time. I have had too many cases where scope was discussed for a long time and the budget turned out to be nowhere close.

requirements.title: Requirements
requirements.description: The clearer the requirements are, the smoother the cooperation is, and the less room there is for unnecessary friction.

communication.title: Communication
communication.description: Be direct and sincere. If it is not a fit, saying so clearly is much better than disappearing.

contact.title: Contact
contact.availabilityDescription: You can message me anytime, 24/7. I reply when I see it. I am usually online on weekdays, while weekends are more family time and I do not check my phone as often.
contact.briefDescription: Including budget, goals, and key requirements is much more effective than only asking “how much would it cost?”.

Sponsor.heading: Support My Work
Sponsor.description: I sincerely thank the wonderful people who support my open source work. Your support helps me continue creating and maintaining open source projects.
